feedback.text: I support the goals of the Digital Fairness Act, but I think it should also protect people from losing access to video games they have already bought. Many publishers shut down games by turning off servers and actively blocking access to experiences that we (customers) paid for them. This is wrong, cruel, and unfair because people lose what they PAID for and cannot enjoy the product anymore. The Act should make sure that once a game is sold, publishers cannot permanently take it away from players.
